segments.text: The future of human space exploration is being driven by what we can discover and accomplish
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: on the moon.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: And with NASA's confirmation of ice existing at the lunar South Pole, the critical task
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: of finding and mapping where water exists, what form it is in, and where it came from,
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: can now begin.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Leading us on that journey will be NASA's first mobile robotic mission on the moon, known
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: as Viper, the volatile investigating polar exploration rover.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: It will be delivered to the nobile region of the South Pole as part of NASA's commercial
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: lunar payload services initiative.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: This region sits just outside the western rim of nobile crater, and covers an area of
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: 36 square miles.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: As the first ever resource mapping mission on the surface of another celestial body, Viper
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: will roam the surface equipped with three science instruments and a drill to detect and analyze
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: various lunar soil environments at a range of depths and temperatures.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The rover will venture into permanently shadowed craters, some of the coldest spots in the
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: solar system, where ice reserves have been preserved for billions of years.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: NASA had four critical parameters when choosing a landing site for Viper, available sunlight,
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: earth visibility for communications from the moon to the earth, data showing the potential
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: presence of water and other resources, and terrain that is well suited for Viper to navigate.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The area to the west of nobile crater met these conditions.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Once on the surface, Viper's mission will last 100 days and cover between 10 to 15
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: miles.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: And while a baseline traverse through the nobile region has been identified for the rover,
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: the scientific discoveries Viper makes along the way will actually influence where the
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: mission team sends it next, so its planned route will most likely change.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: During its travels, Viper will visit at least six locations where data suggests ice could
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: be found.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: By helping determine the locations of where water and other resources exist, Viper's findings
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: will help pave the way for future landing sites under NASA's Artemis program.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The prospects of achieving a long-term human presence on our moon never look so bright.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00
